medications: Naproxen 250 mg as needed with food (arthritis pain)
health.mental_health: Since being laid off from a long-term job and retraining into policing, Jordan has had persistent stress and sleep disruption, with occasional panic symptoms before evaluations and night shifts; they keep this mostly private due to stigma at work.
recent_visits: Gastroenterology follow-up at Hospital das Clínicas (medication review for reflux)
Orthopedist consult in Vila Mariana (knee and hand stiffness; imaging ordered)
Primary care visit at UBS (blood pressure check and lab work)
Physiotherapy sessions for knee strengthening (weekly for 6 weeks)

finance.debt: Car financing and revolving credit card balance accumulated during the layoff period; also a small payroll-deducted loan used to cover retraining costs and rent while transitioning careers.
investments: Small private pension plan (PGBL) started after entering public service
recent_transactions: Paid R$ 1.250 toward credit card balance after receiving overtime
Monthly rent transfer (R$ 3.200) for apartment in Santa Cecília
Purchased ergonomic boots and knee brace for duty (R$ 680)
Sent R$ 600 to help a parent with medications

relationships: Partner: Camila Rocha (together 6 years; supportive but strained by shift work and finances)
Adult child: Matheus Larsson (lives in Campinas; occasional conflict about Jordan’s career change)
Mother: Dona Elza Larsson (late 70s; needs periodic financial help and accompaniment to appointments)
Close friend: Sgt. Renata Almeida (academy cohort; trusted confidant)
Former colleague network from prior long-term job (kept at a distance after layoff)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile online; uses Instagram mostly for cycling photos and São Paulo street photography, avoids posting in uniform, and keeps accounts private with a small follower list.
private_conversations: Discussing non-binary identity and how open to be at work and with extended family
Arguments with partner about debt repayment and whether to move to a cheaper neighborhood
Concerns about being judged as 'too old' in the police force and fear of failing fitness standards
Family discussions about mother’s health needs and who will take responsibility

schedule.work_hours: Rotating shifts (12x36); occasional mandatory overtime and weekend coverage
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check at UBS every 2 months
Monthly meeting with a financial counselor (online, evenings)
Biweekly language practice group (Portuguese/English) for report writing and career development
upcoming_events: Quarterly physical fitness evaluation at the training center
Follow-up orthopedist appointment to review imaging results
Family lunch in Mooca for mother’s birthday
Union meeting about shift allocations and staffing
schedule.daily_routine: On shift days Jordan wakes early, takes reflux medication, eats a light breakfast, commutes by metro when possible, and packs low-acid snacks; on off days they run errands, help their mother, and try to fit in cycling or physiotherapy exercises.

professional.company: Polícia Militar do Estado de São Paulo (PMESP) — patrol unit assigned within the capital
projects: Community policing rounds with local comerciantes and residents’ associations
Body-worn camera compliance and report-quality improvement initiative
Field training refreshers focused on de-escalation and procedural updates
professional.performance_issues: Generally solid performance, but Jordan has had occasional fatigue-related mistakes in paperwork and slower recovery after foot pursuits; they worry supervisors may link this to age and stress from the career transition.
workplace_relationships: Supportive mentor relationship with a senior cabo who helps navigate unit culture
Tense dynamic with one supervisor who makes dismissive comments about Jordan’s age and identity
Strong peer bond with academy cohort members who also changed careers later in life

hobbies: Weekend cycling on Ciclovia do Rio Pinheiros
Cooking lighter Brazilian meals (grilled fish, rice, beans, salads)
Short hikes in Parque Estadual da Cantareira when off duty
habits: Avoids coffee and spicy foods during flare-ups, but sometimes breaks this on night shifts
Uses antacids in the patrol bag for sudden reflux
Occasional social drinking after stressful shifts (kept discreet)
Keeps a private journal to manage stress and identity-related worries
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in Santa Cecília with their partner; they considered downsizing after the layoff and still keep a strict budget while stabilizing in the new career.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Prefers smaller, earlier dinners to reduce reflux; limits fried foods and citrus, aims for higher fiber meals, and tries to stay hydrated during shifts.
